Melastoma nitidum is a plant species that has been traditionally used in medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Melastoma nitidum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections. In traditional medicine, Melastoma nitidum is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of ointments and creams.
